key_insight: A physics-informed CNN-LSTM can forecast long-term photoluminescence degradation of perovskites from early time-series data, enabling faster stability assessment on unseen materials.
engineering_relevance: This approach can shorten experimental qualification cycles for semiconductor materials by reducing the need for full-duration ageing tests while preserving interpretability.
method: Collected 167 metal halide perovskite samples spanning diverse compositions and crystallinities.
Stressed samples with heat and illumination while recording more than 86k photoluminescence spectra.
Used physics-informed featurization plus a hybrid CNN-LSTM to predict PL stability trajectories and identify influential stability descriptors.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on samples unseen during training and outperformed baseline benchmarks while generalizing across material groups.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the physics-informed features and could be less reliable for materials or degradation regimes outside the training distribution.
paper_title: Physics-informed time-series forecasting of perovskite photoluminescence stability
